message:
perf(bench): Expanded benchmarks for indexing, search, and caching

Adds comprehensive benchmarking infrastructure for performance
regression detection and optimization guidance:

Index Performance (index_perf.rs):
- Minor improvements to benchmark accuracy
- Consistent warm-up patterns across test runs

Runtime Performance (runtime_perf.rs):
- Query parsing benchmarks at various complexity levels
- Search execution timing with different result sizes
- Filter application overhead measurement
- Memory allocation tracking during search
- BM25 scoring computation benchmarks

Cache Microbenchmarks (cache_micro.rs - NEW):
- LRU cache hit/miss patterns
- Cache eviction timing
- Concurrent access patterns
- Memory pressure scenarios
- Cache warming strategies

Benchmark Configuration:
- Uses criterion for statistical rigor
- Configurable sample sizes for CI vs local runs
- Baseline comparison support for regression detection
- JSON output for automated processing

These benchmarks establish performance baselines to prevent
regressions and guide future optimization work. The cache
microbenchmarks are particularly important for understanding
query result caching behavior under load.

Run with: cargo bench --bench <name>
Compare: cargo bench -- --save-baseline <name>

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
